reasoning: The informal statement asserts that ℚ (as an additive group) has no proper subgroups of finite index. The Lean statement quantifies over all `AddSubgroup ℚ` with `H ≠ ⊤` (i.e., proper subgroups) and concludes `H.index = 0`. In Mathlib, `AddSubgroup.index` is defined via `Nat.card (G ⧸ H)`, which returns 0 precisely when the quotient is infinite — i.e., when H has infinite index. Thus `H ≠ ⊤ → H.index = 0` is logically equivalent to "every proper subgroup has infinite index," which matches the informal claim exactly.
